title: Twig — About
answer:
Interested in reading without even the slightest spoiler? Click here.

The year is 1921, and a little over a century has passed since a great mind unraveled the underpinnings of life itself. Every week, it seems, the papers announce great advances, solving the riddle of immortality, successfully reviving the dead, the cloning of living beings, or blending

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.1
question: At one point Sylvester is called Sy and later he’s called Sly. Is that intentional and simply characters calling him by different nicknames or a typo?
answer: It is intentional.

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.1
question: Excellent catch from GreatWyrmGold mentioned on the Reddit: An academy is referenced in Wildbow’s WOG comments in a Worm chapter: Sovrano Academy: Regent. Very different person, same power as demonstrated in the Parasite arc, not the
answer: No relation between this Academy and Sovrano Academy. Sovrano was my attempt at writing a ‘superheroes in high school’ thing with an emphasis on students in training to become supervillains.

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.3
question:
And we get yet another reference to sets of 4. I see it as:

Helen- 1st man, shoots and feels no regrets

Sy- 2nd man, complains, shoots anyway, no regrets

Jamie- 3rd man, complains, shoots anyway, deep regrets

Gordon- 4th man, refuses
answer: Falls apart when you figure that Gordon ‘shot’ the snake charmer, no?

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.5
question:
I liked the conversations as well. It was fun and smooth, but doesn’t necessarily feel like the dialogue of 11 and 12 year old children. It’s obvious in Sylvester’s narration and when the gang converses amongst themselves.

My question had
answer: Keep in mind that it’s a different generation. Children didn’t (and in this AU/AH, don’t) have a language of their own, and were expected to act and talk like adults in many respects.

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.6
question: Good chapter, but I didn’t understand the deal with the chest and the show laces. What did Sly actually did?
answer: Deadfall. Heavy bit of wood bristling with various spiky things ready to get pulled down on someone coming through the door.

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.7
question:
Typo Thread!

“How are you doing, Sy?” I asked. “How did you do it? Are you okay?”

Is Sy asking himself…?
answer: Yes. Yes he is.

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.8
question: Hm. The psychology terminology Sylvester uses in the first part of the chapter is interesting. I don’t think a lot of it came into real use until around the nineteen-fifties in our world. Whatever deficiences in neuroscientific knowledge
answer: In the case of Dissonance, if nothing else, it’s rooted in an old term and just makes sense as a word for what it is.

title: WoG · Cat out of the Bag 2.10
question: Well, we’ve seen Sy do the opposite, too. Like throwing Helen in the way of his attacker to avoid getting hurt (killed).
answer: That wasn’t a defensive move. It was an offensive move.

title: WoG · Lips Sealed 3.5
question: I have also noticed some of this. I feel it has been appropriately lampshaded, though. I almost appreciate it, actually, the recognition that this kind of stuff happens. Wildbow is an exceptional writer when it comes to sexism in general,
answer: Outside of the S9, though… there’s quite a few. If you add them up across all stories, I have more monstrous girls than monstrous men.

title: WoG · Lips Sealed 3.9
question:
This is an odd paragraph:

“I caught up to Helen quickly enough. My hand found hers. As she stumbled, I caught her with my shoulder in her armpit, bracing her with my entire body, scraping a shin against a stair while the both of us
answer: Not of stumbling, but losing her grip.

title: WoG · Lamb to the Slaughter – 6.5
question: That’s kinda like saying that a shape with four corners and equal sides is a square, not a rectangle. You’re technically correct in that they were referred to as fae in Pact, but they’re really just different words for the same thing with
answer: Elves are different from Fey. Word of God. 😉

title: WoG · Lamb to the Slaughter – 6.9
question: I think he meant “they can fix the fact that I can’t be attracted to you”.
answer: Nope.

title: WoG · Lamb to the Slaughter – 6.9
question: Jaime has basically had his block and tackle removed, so to speak. That can be fixed… Although, I really don’t want to think about donors.
answer: Sy was saying they could fix Jamie’s attraction to him.

title: WoG · Tooth and Nail – 7.13
question: First and foremost, I love everything you come up with Wildbow, I really do. I’m sorry if I’ve missed the explanation in a previous set of comments, but I really just want to know what exactly a birthing saw is. We’ve seen this guy a few
answer:
Before chainsaws were used to cut wood, they were invented to assist in the process of childbirth.

It’s a chainsaw.

title: WoG · Enemy (Arc 9)
question:
Awesome new art. Is that Gordon & Hubris with the primordial? Or a regular warbeast? Gordon always had a connection to other experiments…

On the chapter: Oh fuck the hell no. No no no no. You didn’t just unleash a primordial pandemia on
answer: Thanks for the typo notes Seth. Just intended as a regular warbeast.

title: WoG · In Sheep’s Clothing – 10.3
question: Ah aheheh. Interesting. When the story started, I really disliked Sy for giving Lillian such a hard time. But now, I understand. Lillian is a Lamb. Craig isn’t, and I don’t want him around at all.
answer: Duncan.

title: WoG · In Sheep’s Clothing – 10.9
question: Mcormick’s wife is 18 but has a 6 year old daughter?
answer: -Looks- eighteen.

title: WoG · In Sheep’s Clothing – 10.17
question: Five times Sy’s size, not five times his height. Size is Height * Depth * Width. Assuming similar proportions, he’d just be about 1.7 times Sy’s height (the cube root of 5). We also know that he’s shorter than his father, who is in turn
answer: Yep

title: WoG · In Sheep’s Clothing – 10.18
question: Wait what? The Baron took out an eye and Sy couldn’t fix it (because of the Baron). I thought he was booby trapping the *remaining* eye. But then when he “uses” it… isn’t he now entirely without sight? That’s gonna make the rest of the
answer: Sy got a non-functional replacement from Simon & the other Warrick doctor artistes. He mangled his replacement.

title: WoG · In Sheep’s Clothing – 10.20
question:
Yesssssss even more delicious Symie feelings for the future!

Now that I am (for the moment) done rolling around in feelings, I have to say that I love that there was really no need to explain how Sy, alone, got new eyes and muscles for
answer: Just eyes, no muscles.

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.12
question: Hey Wildbow, love your work. There’s a slight continuity error in this chapter. Sy asks Helen for a comb, but immediately afterwards Helen is tranquilized on the floor.
answer: That’s just how Helen operates.

title: WoG · Black Sheep – 13.1 (Lamb)
question: Typo thread! Shouldn’t “Abby emulated him, matching the tilt of his head” be Lara?
answer: Nope. Abby copied to see Lara’s eyes. Added another bit to the following sentence for clarification.

title: WoG · Taking Root 1.7
question:
I am loving how manipulative Sy is, very Tattletale-esque. And I love how his worst nightmare is Helen, someone he cannot read or manipulate.

Quick question, the nickname seems to jump between Sy and Sly. Is this intentional?
answer: It is. They eventually settle into the one nickname.

title: WoG · Black Sheep – 13.11
question:
I feel like Sylvester’s self-repression is so devastating for Jamie because it shows that Syl really didn’t love New Jamie as much as Old Jamie, even after Jamie defected for him.

When Old Jamie confessed to Sylvester, Sylvester’s
answer: When Sylvester said “They can fix that” way back in Radham, he meant they could fix Jamie.

title: WoG · Twig
question: I would like to know the genre of the work before i start reading if that could be provided please?
answer: Biopunk. Alternate history: What if Victor Frankenstein was real and started a scientific revolution that was extremely lopsided toward biology? (Except his name wasn’t Frankenstein)

title: WoG · Thicker than Water – 14.6
question:
Typo thread?

” only what Marv and Jamie had been able to impart ”

should be Mary and Jamie
answer: Marv is right.

title: WoG · Bitter Pill – 15.14
question:
Okay, I’ll admit to bring totally confused. This whole reveal, where the nobles are actually just ordinary people turned into glorifies experiments. How is this a surprise anyone? What else could they have been?

The only thing I can see
answer: It undermines the legitimacy of a line of leadership extending back since before Wollstone. The Crown has people who are loyal and who recognize or believe in the blood-given right to rule. There’s the idea that these people were the cream of the crop to begin with, in breeding and wealth, in esteem and in talent, and then they were made better by cutting

title: WoG · Head over Heels – 16.8
question:
Since the Dr was specifically building that bug for Sylvester, I suspect his loss has more to do with whatever chemicals he is being pumped with that counteract Wyvern than a loss from general mental wear and tear.

Omg, That professor
answer: Keep in mind that Jessie is wearing a quarantine suit, and the doctor has no cue/reason to think that she’s Jessie. He’s deducing that this vocal character in a quarantine suit must be a lamb, and by process of elimination…

title: WoG · Gut Feeling – 17.1
question: Helen mentioned “Jessie” when she and Ashton met Sylvester earlier. Was this a mistake, or does Helen know things she’s not sharing with the rest of the Lambs?
answer: Lipreading.

title: WoG · Gut Feeling – 17.18
question:
Bzzt, wrong. All these chapters have it written “Ibott” (with 0 instances of “Ibbot”):

Weird ones:

That’s just from the first couple google pages of looking for it, but I do believe my case stands.

Some commenters started typoing it
answer: Blame Sy’s memory.
